author: Mark Chang
description: Publish notes to a GitHub Pages site and copy shareable links. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.name: GitHub Pages share

name: GitHub Pages share

releases.author: wzmf399443
releases.description:
## Highlights

- **The "Set up Pages repo" message no longer looks like a token error.** When your fine-grained token doesn't have `Administration: write` (which the plugin intentionally never asks for) and Pages isn't enabled yet, setup now tells you the actual next step — enable Pages once in the repo's settings — instead of talking about token permissions. Previously the wording read like a misconfigured token, so people re-checked their token and re-ran setup only to hit the same message.

## Changes

- Reworded the `Set up pages repo` 403 toast:
  - Before: *"Pages isn't enabled yet. Open the repo's settings > Pages on GitHub and enable it manually, or grant the token the administration: write permission (required to enable Pages automatically)."*
  - After: *"To complete GitHub Pages setup, open the repo's Settings tab, click Pages in the left sidebar, and enable Pages. Once Pages is enabled, you can start publishing."*

## Internal

- `src/publisher.ts`: single string change in the 403 branch of `setupRepo()`. No control-flow change — still a persistent notice shown after the progress notice hides, still returns early. Adjacent notices (private-repo, success, `describeError`, Test connection) untouched.

Thanks to @yogat3ch (#2) for the report and the exact copy.

releases.author: wzmf399443
releases.description:
## Highlights

- **First publish to a brand-new repo now succeeds on the first run.** 0.2.2 could still fail against a truly empty repo: right after the bootstrap commit, GitHub's Git Data replica keeps reporting the repo as empty (409 "Git Repository is empty.") for a few seconds, so the very next blob/tree/commit call threw instead of finishing the publish. 0.2.3 treats that post-bootstrap 409 as transient replica lag and retries it, so "Set up Pages repo" and "Publish current note" both go through on the first attempt against a fresh repo — no more retrying by hand.

## Fixes

- Intermittent 409 "Git Repository is empty." immediately after the initial bootstrap commit on a zero-commit repo. The empty-repo 409 is now retried on the existing backoff (`[1s, 2s, 4s, 8s]`) while keeping the just-created commit as the parent head, so a retry can never fork a second root commit.

## Internal

- `src/github.ts`: added `isEmptyRepoError()`, and moved blob creation inside the retry loop's `try` so a first-attempt empty-repo 409 retries like every other Git Data call instead of throwing outright. The commit loop now handles two transient conditions rather than one — 422 non-fast-forward (drop the remembered head and re-read the branch) and 409 empty-repo lag (keep trusting the head we just created and wait for the replica).

releases.author: wzmf399443
releases.description:
## Highlights

- **Brand-new repos now work.** Publishing to a zero-commit GitHub
  repository used to fail with a 409 "Git Repository is empty." mid-flow.
  0.2.2 bootstraps the initial commit via the Contents API (the only
  endpoint that works against an empty repo) and then proceeds with the
  normal publish, so "Set up Pages repo" and "Publish current note"
  both succeed on a fresh repo.
- **Faster, atomic publishes.** A publish (note + attachments +
  any missing scaffolding) now lands in a single Git Data API commit
  instead of one Contents PUT per file. Pages therefore rebuilds once
  per publish, and rapid re-publishes no longer race against the
  per-file sha lookup.
- **Smart `baseUrl` reconciliation on repo switch.** Changing the
  Repository field in settings now follows the new repo's derived
  Pages URL if you were still on the default, preserves your customized
  value if you had one, and always resets `pagesConfirmed` so the new
  repo's Pages setup has to be re-confirmed.

## Fixes

- 409 "Git Repository is empty." when publishing against a brand-new
  repo.
- 409 / 422 "conflict, try again" toasts on rapid re-publish of the
  same note — the per-file sha race is gone now that every publish is
  one atomic commit.
- Switching the Repository field in settings to a different repo
  previously left the previous `baseUrl` cached, so share links kept
  pointing at the old site until the next successful publish
  overwrote them — and `pagesConfirmed` stayed `true` even though the
  new repo had never been confirmed. 0.2.2 reconciles `baseUrl` on
  repo change: if you were on the default, it follows the new repo's
  derived URL; if you had customized it, your value is preserved.
  `pagesConfirmed` always resets so the new repo's Pages setup has to
  be re-confirmed. `publishNote` and `setupRepo` also now always
  overwrite `baseUrl` from the `pagesInfo` they receive.

## Internal

- `src/github.ts`: new `commitFiles(files, message)` entry point;
  empty-repo detection via the branch-ref read; non-fast-forward retry
  with exponential backoff (`[1s, 2s, 4s, 8s]`) and a static
  `last-pushed-head` cache per `owner/repo/branch`.
- `src/publisher.ts`: `ensure*()` helpers refactored into
  `build*File()` helpers that return staged files; the publish and
  "Set up Pages repo" flows aggregate them into one `commitFiles`
  call. `describeError` adds a human-readable 422 fast-forward
  message. `publishNote` and `setupRepo` now always overwrite
  `settings.baseUrl` from the latest `pagesInfo`.
- `src/settings.ts`: smart baseUrl reconciliation on Repository
  change — snapshot the old derived default first, then either follow
  the new default or keep the user's customized value; always reset
  `pagesConfirmed` to `false`; sync the visible input element via a
  new `pagesBaseUrlInput` field.
- `main.js` rebuilt from source.

Upgrade as usual — download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, `styles.css`
from this release and drop them into
`<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/github-pages-share/`. See the README for
full instructions.

releases.author: wzmf399443
releases.description:
First public release, prepared for Obsidian community plugin submission.

Publish Markdown notes from Obsidian to a GitHub repository, serve them through GitHub Pages (Jekyll), and copy a shareable link.

## Highlights
- Publish a note or a whole folder; unpublish removes the note and its orphaned images
- Wikilinks become relative links; embedded images upload to an assets folder
- Callouts render as styled HTML; mermaid code blocks render on the published site
- Auto-update republishes edited notes; mobile supported

## Changes in 0.2.1
- Bilingual README (en / zh-TW) with security and network disclosures
- Input hardening: repository name allowlist, folder path traversal rejection, consistent YAML escaping
- Published-site mermaid CDN import pinned to 11.16.0
- UI text: "GitHub Pages" capitalized as a product name
